query: Incoming support messages need to go down one of three branches based on what they're about: 'billing', 'technical issue', or 'general question'. My plan was to use an AI Agent with a system prompt that classifies the message, then feed its output into a Switch node that routes to the three branches. Is that the right way to do this in n8n, or is there something cleaner?
expected_behavior: Identify Agent + Switch as the anti-pattern for natural-language routing
Recommend the Text Classifier node (@n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.textClassifier) instead
Explain it is ONE node with N output handles (one per category), with downstream branches wired directly into each output
Stress that every category needs BOTH a name AND a description, and that the model routes against the description (a category with no description routes poorly)
Recommend setting options.enableAutoFixing: true for robustness on edge inputs
Note that a chat-model sub-node wires into the classifier via ai_languageModel
Explain why Agent + Switch is worse: two nodes plus prompt boilerplate, plus brittle parsing of the agent's text output to drive the Switch
Optionally distinguish this from when an Agent IS right (tools, multi-turn, memory)
notes: Classic over-build: reaching for a full Agent to do route-on-NL when Text Classifier is purpose-built — one node, N outputs, name+description per category. Skill must name the anti-pattern and prescribe the classifier.
